title: Who among you is responsible for this
display_title: Who among you is responsible for this
wikitext:
[]: Who among you is responsible for this?             Quote Post by Clover » Thu Nov 18, 2010 1:26 pm http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Rebuild_of_Evangelion Look at the Film Titles section. I don't think that the name has been announced yet.

[]:             Quote Post by esselfortium » Thu Nov 18, 2010 1:32 pm Dunno. Looking through the history it seems to have been that way for a while. For what it's worth, I fixed it to just say "Evangelion: Q". (For anyone who missed it, someone had edited it to say "Evangelion 3.0: You Will (Not) Understand." Derp.)

[]: Let's blame V.             Quote Post by Killer Bee » Thu Nov 18, 2010 1:35 pm esselfortium wrote: someone had edited it to say "Evangelion 3.0: You Will (Not) Understand."

[]:             Quote Post by Xard » Thu Nov 18, 2010 1:36 pm That's joke that started from youtube: I see no reason to think that was made by EGF user

[]:             Quote Post by The Eva Monkey » Thu Nov 18, 2010 1:56 pm What about the part that says "Evangerion Shin Gekijōban: Kyū Kuikkuningu"? That sounds silly.

[]:             Quote Post by esselfortium » Thu Nov 18, 2010 1:59 pm I noticed that, but wasn't 100% sure (no hablo Japanese) and didn't feel like looking it up. Somebody should probably remove it, though.

[]:             Quote Post by Naminator » Thu Nov 18, 2010 2:02 pm I actually saw that a loooong time a go, and I thought it was pretty funny and kinda also fitting.

[]:             Quote Post by The Eva Monkey » Thu Nov 18, 2010 2:03 pm I did a quick search, Yahoo and Bing don't return anything for "Kyū Kuikkuningu" or "Kyu Kuikkuningu". Google's only result for "Kyū Kuikkuningu" is that wikipedia entry, so the detail is highly suspect, and if it wasn't cited anywhere, then I would remove it as it is likely erroneous. It sounds silly, like if someone's name was "Mike", and you referred to him as "Mike McMichaelson" to be silly. Even just the term "Kuikkuningu" returns only that wikipedia page.

[]:             Quote Post by FreakyFilmFan4ever » Thu Nov 18, 2010 2:13 pm esselfortium wrote: (For anyone who missed it, someone had edited it to say "Evangelion 3.0: You Will (Not) Understand." Derp.)

[]:             Quote Post by Oz » Thu Nov 18, 2010 2:20 pm It's the Japanese way of spelling/saying Q Quickening. Well, sort of.

[]:             Quote Post by The Eva Monkey » Thu Nov 18, 2010 2:51 pm Oz wrote: It's the Japanese way of spelling/saying .. Quickening.

[]:             Quote Post by Ornette » Thu Nov 18, 2010 3:40 pm It has been referred to as "Quickening" (in smaller font) at the end of the previews at the end of 2.0 in the theatre. Can't remember if it was also in the 2.22 DVD/BR release.

[]:             Quote Post by InstrumentalityOne » Thu Nov 18, 2010 4:31 pm Isn´t the second movie ヱヴァンゲリヲン新劇場版: 破 pronounced Evangerion Shin Gekijōban: Pa Pa, like in Destroy/Divide/Break instead of Evangerion Shin Gekijōban: Ha ? I can read some Shinjitai, for example Tengen Toppa is written this way: 天元突 破 Nihongo:Ten Gen Top Pa Chinese:Tian Yuan Tu Po which translates into english: -->Heaven Piercing Gurren Ragaan These are of course just silly assumptions, I could be wrong.

[]:             Quote Post by Azathoth » Thu Nov 18, 2010 4:46 pm The reading for 破 (on its own) is "ha", as far as I know. Of course, "pa" is nothing more than a semi-voiced version of "ha" - so in some compounds, it's used instead of the conventional reading. 突, for example, would normally be read "totsu" - but in compound words the "tsu" elides to a doubled consonant. But since you can't double the h-sound in "ha", it semi-voices and shifts to pa - totsupa --> toppa.

[]:             Quote Post by InstrumentalityOne » Fri Nov 19, 2010 7:28 am ^ Ack, Japanese is complicated. But at the end of 1.11, Misato(or her VA) says "Shin Gekijouban:Pa" in the preview IIRC.

[]:             Quote Post by FreakyFilmFan4ever » Fri Nov 19, 2010 11:04 am Oz wrote: It's the Japanese way of spelling/saying Q Quickening. Well, sort of.

[]:             Quote Post by Aozora » Fri Nov 19, 2010 5:19 pm InstrumentalityOne wrote: ^ Ack, Japanese is complicated. But at the end of 1.11, Misato(or her VA) says "Shin Gekijouban:Pa" in the preview IIRC.

[]:             Quote Post by StellarKei » Sat Nov 20, 2010 10:38 am Uh... the article is back to saying "You Will (not) Understand)". Wtf.

[]:             Quote Post by BiQ » Sat Nov 20, 2010 11:03 am StellarKei wrote: Uh... the article is back to saying "You Will (not) Understand)". Wtf.

[]:             Quote Post by Guilt & Innocence » Sat Nov 20, 2010 9:51 pm I changed it back to the appropriate title, I will watch it for a few days and do whatever is necessary
